notes: Subject of the advisory report. Split into HP Inc (consumer) and HPE (enterprise) in 2015. HPE carries the server/HA business forward.
Author of the flash report. Acquired by Harte-Hanks in 2008; brand later absorbed into Spiceworks Ziff Davis.
Competitor discussed. Report noted IBM understood HA technologies but had not unified HA across four hardware platforms or pursued end-to-end beyond custom Global Services projects.
Competitor discussed. Described as focused on single-box HA, poor-to-fair in clustering, lacking global services and end-to-end concept. Acquired by Oracle in 2010.
Competitor discussed. HA thrust limited to rack-mounted clusters. Digital services capability not widely deployed in HA. Acquired by HP in 2002.
NT fault-tolerance provider. Aberdeen recommended HP acquire Marathon for FT-on-NT capability. HP did not act; Marathon was later acquired by Stratus Technologies circa 2005.
Mentioned as a key relationship partner for HP's end-to-end HA strategy.
Mentioned as a key relationship partner for HP's end-to-end HA strategy. Acquired by Dell in 2016.
Referenced as the provider of the NT base platform. HP's NT HA story depended on improving upon Microsoft's base platform.
Referenced for September 1998 clustering white paper that rated HP's single-Unix-system availability as average.

successor: Compaq (1997); then HP (2002); technology lives on as HPE NonStop
notes: Division of Compaq at time of report. Deep HA expertise but focused on selling to installed base. Technology continues as HPE NonStop.
Referenced indirectly via Compaq's Digital services capability. Acquired by Compaq in 1998.
